sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I'm stuck trying to figure out the target configuration. Let me try a different approach: maybe the small yellow markers indicate which positions should have which colors. Let me look at the markers more carefully.
The markers are at:
- (17,10): above and left of the top-left corner
- (17,16): above the gap between first and second top row blocks
- (23,10): left of the first left column block
- (23,16): left of the gap between first and second left column blocks... wait, there's no block at (23,16)
Actually, let me reconsider the frame layout. The frame has:
- Top row: 7 blocks at (19,12), (19,18), (19,24), (19,30), (19,36), (19,42), (19,48)
- Right column: 3 blocks at (25,48), (31,48), (37,48)
- Bottom row: 7 blocks at (43,48), (43,42), (43,36), (43,30), (43,24), (43,18), (43,12)
- Left column: 3 blocks at (37,12), (31,12), (25,12)
The markers are at (17,10), (17,16), (23,10), (23,16). Let me map these to the nearest frame positions:
- (17,10) → (19,12) - top-left corner (position 0)
- (17,16) → (19,18) - second in top row (position 1)
- (23,10) → (25,12) - first in left column (position 17)
- (23,16) → (19,18) or (25,12)? Neither is very close.
Hmm, this mapping isn't clean. Let me try a different approach.
Maybe the markers indicate a 2x2 pattern that should appear at the top-left of the frame. The 2x2 region at the top-left would include:
- (19,12) - top-left corner
- (19,18) - second in top row
- (25,12) - first in left column
- (25,18) - but there's no block at (25,18)!
Wait, there's no block at (25,18). The left column blocks are at (25,12), (31,12), (37,12), and the top row blocks are at (19,12), (19,18), (19,24), etc. So (25,18) is empty space inside the frame.
Hmm, let me try yet another approach. Maybe the markers don't indicate target positions but rather indicate that the blocks at those positions should be clicked. But clicking on colored blocks does nothing...
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the problem. Maybe I'm overthinking this. Let me look at the initial state and the state after clicking red:
Initial: w, g, S, b, p, Y, g, p, b, S, g, p, S, b, b, w, w, g, p, S
After red (clockwise 1): g, S, b, p, Y, g, p, b, S, g, p, S, b, b, w, w, g, p, S, w
The Y block moved from position 5 to position 4. What if the goal is to get Y to a specific position?
Let me look at the markers again. They're all yellow (Y). Maybe they indicate positions where Y should be. But there are 4 markers and only 1 Y block, so that doesn't work directly.
Unless... the markers indicate that Y should visit all 4 positions over time, and the level is complete when Y has visited all marked positions?
Or maybe the markers indicate that the blocks at those positions should all be the same color?
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me just try rotating the blocks clockwise many times and see if the level completes at some point. I'll batch 17 red clicks and see what happens.
Actually wait, I should be more careful. Let me first try a few rotations and see if anything special happens (like the top bar changing or the level completing).

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# and see if anything special happens
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 5} for _ in range(3)])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
